name: repo_root
observations: Course + practice repo for Claude Code mastery
Top-level sections: 00-materials, 01-small-project, 02-mcp, 03-auto-simplify, 04-uiux, 05-subagents, 06-github-actions
No shared build system — each section is self-contained
200 files, ~296k tokens packed

name: 00-materials
observations: Reference material: official Claude Code docs (42 doc files) + 20-section course transcripts
docs/ covers hooks, MCP, sub-agents, settings, skills, GitHub Actions, checkpointing, scheduled tasks, output styles, fast mode
course/ maps each section to evidence of practice and identifies gaps
00-docs-plan.md and 00-course-plan.md contain gap analysis with priority-ordered practice plans
No executable code — purely instructional content

name: 01-small-project
observations: Next.js 14 + Prisma (SQLite) + Vitest app — a UI code generation assistant
Entry points: src/app/page.tsx (project list), src/app/[projectId]/page.tsx (editor), src/app/api/chat/route.ts (AI chat endpoint)
Tech: Next.js App Router, Prisma ORM, shadcn/ui components, Vitest + React Testing Library
CLAUDE.md at project root provides Claude with architecture + Prisma schema reference via @-syntax
Absolute-path hook workaround: settings.example.json uses $PWD placeholder; init-claude.js substitutes and writes settings.local.json
npm run setup installs deps + runs init-claude.js to generate settings.local.json with absolute paths
Three server actions: create-project.ts, get-project.ts, get-projects.ts — all in src/actions/

name: 03-auto-simplify
observations: Demonstrates PostToolUse + UserPromptSubmit hook pair for auto-enforcing code simplification
PostToolUse/counter.py: fires after Edit|Write|NotebookEdit, increments /tmp/cc-edits-{session_id}, exits 2 with stderr message at threshold=5
UserPromptSubmit/gate.py: reads prompt, blocks further prompts if threshold reached and /simplify not yet invoked, detects /simplify invocations to reset cycle
State stored in /tmp/ with session_id-scoped files — state is lost when session ends
Uses $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR env var in settings.json for portable hook paths — avoids absolute path problem
hook event PostToolUse with exit 2 does NOT prevent the file write — the write already happened; exit 2 signals Claude to take corrective action

name: 04-uiux
observations: Multi-agent UI pipeline enforcing 7-stage decomposition: IntentSpec→LayoutSpec→TokenSpec→ComponentSpec→assembly→CritiqueReport→repair
6 custom commands: ui-brief, ui-build, ui-critique, ui-layout, ui-repair, ui-style — all accept $ARGUMENTS
6 subagents in .claude/subagents/: ux-reasoner, layout-planner, token-architect, component-mapper, codegen-engineer, visual-critic
5 JSON schemas in schemas/: intent-spec, layout-spec, token-spec, component-spec, critique-report — output contracts for each stage
5 PostToolUse hooks in hooks/: run-typecheck.sh, run-axe.sh, detect-duplicate-components.sh, enforce-token-usage.sh, run-visual-diff.sh, run-storybook-smoke.sh
run-typecheck.sh: checks package.json for typecheck script, falls back to build — exits 0 always (non-blocking)
CLAUDE.md enforces anti-generic rules, token discipline, and layer responsibilities
repomix-output/ contains pre-packed repo.xml + summary.md for whole-repo context without re-packing
repomix.config.json configures repomix to output to repomix-output/

name: 05-subagents
observations: Portable ~/.claude/ dotfiles for multi-project reuse — agents, skills, hooks, global CLAUDE.md
install.sh: creates symlinks from dotfiles repo into ~/.claude/ — settings.json NOT symlinked (must copy + edit paths manually)
4 global agents: orchestrator, codebase-analyst, mcp-manager, task-runner
4 skills: repomix, mcp-knowledge-graph, sequential-thinking, nuxt
1 hook: context-loader.sh — UserPromptSubmit hook that reads .aim/memory.jsonl and injects entity summaries into session on first prompt
context-loader.sh uses /tmp/claude-kg-{session_id} marker to inject only once per session — not on every prompt
settings.json: alwaysThinkingEnabled=true, effortLevel=low, skipDangerousModePermissionPrompt=true, 13 plugins enabled
3 custom commands: detect-roles.md, init-project.md, startup.md

name: 06-github-actions
observations: GitHub-as-event-bus pattern: @claude in issues/PRs triggers ephemeral Actions runner
claude.yml: triggers on issue_comment, pull_request_review_comment, pull_request_review, issues — all with @claude filter
Uses anthropics/claude-code-action@v1 with claude_code_oauth_token (NOT ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)
oauth token generated via claude setup-token, valid 1 year, covers all workflows on repo
custom_instructions is deprecated in v1 action — use claude_args with --system-prompt, --allowedTools, --max-turns
ci-rust.yml: Rust CI workflow; claude-pr-review.yml: auto PR review on every PR
gh-auth-switch.sh: detects GitHub user from SSH remote, checks/guides gh CLI auth switch for multi-account setups
setup-github-actions.md skill: fully automated setup for multi-account SSH environments
INSIGHTS.md: documents all lessons from gecko-shit multi-user test — critical reference for CI setup

name: orchestrator
observations: 6-phase workflow: clarify→analyze→plan→implement→review→test
Never skips Phase 2 (codebase analysis) — spawns codebase-analyst subagent
Max 7 tasks per plan — requires decomposition if more needed
One task = one commit; never batch; never amend published commits
Spawns mcp-manager for MCP tool calls during implementation
Phase 5 review checks: logic errors, security, convention violations, edge cases

name: codebase-analyst
observations: 3-phase: pack with repomix → sequential-thinking branches → store in knowledge graph
Reads repomix output directly with Read tool — never uses read_repomix_output via mcporter (outputId dies in subprocess mode)
Three required branches: structure, runtime-behaviors, limits-and-failures
Stores to location:project (.aim/memory.jsonl) if .aim/ exists, else globally
Output: Architecture table, Execution Flow sequence, Configuration table, What Breaks Silently table

name: mcp-manager
observations: MCP tool executor with 5-tier fallback chain: gemini-cli→kimi-cli→qwen→codex→mcporter
Writes results to .aim/results/{task_id}.json even on failure
Always returns structured JSON with tool used, status, output

name: task-runner
observations: Generic task delegator: receives role+tool+task_id+task+context, delegates to external AI CLI
Fallback chain: qwen→kimi→codex→claude(inline)
Writes result to .aim/results/{task_id}.json
When claude(inline) is the fallback, executes directly using Read/Edit/Write/Bash tools — does not shell out

name: context-loader.sh
observations: UserPromptSubmit hook — fires on every user prompt
Reads all .aim/memory*.jsonl files, prints first 15 entities with 2 observations each to stdout
Uses /tmp/claude-kg-{session_id} marker so injection only happens once per session
If no .aim/memory.jsonl but .aim/roles.json exists, prints roles summary instead
stdout from UserPromptSubmit hooks is injected into Claude's context as tool result

name: counter.py
observations: PostToolUse hook matching Edit|Write|NotebookEdit
Increments /tmp/cc-edits-{session_id} counter per-session
At THRESHOLD=5: exits 2 with stderr message instructing Claude to invoke simplify skill
If done_file (/tmp/cc-simplified-{session_id}) exists: deletes it, resets counter to 0, exits 0
Exit 2 on PostToolUse does not block the write — it signals Claude to take corrective action in next turn

name: gate.py
observations: UserPromptSubmit hook — fires before Claude processes each prompt
If threshold reached AND done_file not present: blocks prompt with exit 2, stderr message
If prompt contains /simplify: creates done_file to signal completion, allows through
Pairs with counter.py to enforce simplify-after-N-edits workflow

name: gh-auth-switch.sh
observations: Helper script (not a Claude hook) for multi-account GitHub CLI auth management
Three modes: resolve (detect GitHub user from SSH remote), check (verify gh CLI matches), switch (guide auth switch if mismatched)
Uses ssh -T git@{SSH_HOST} to resolve actual GitHub identity from SSH config aliases
Needed because gh CLI ignores SSH config aliases — always uses hosts.yml auth token

name: setup-github-actions.md
observations: Automates full GH Actions setup for multi-account SSH environments
Phase 1: detect GitHub user from SSH remote (no user input required)
Phase 2: check gh CLI auth, guide switch if mismatched
Phase 3: install Claude GitHub App via gh api
Phase 4: generate and commit workflow YAML files
Phase 5: set CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN secret via gh secret set

name: mcp-knowledge-graph_skill
observations: Documents the coeus MCP server's 10 tools for persistent project knowledge graphs
Key storage mechanic: every write rewrites the entire file — keep graphs focused
No concurrency: two sessions writing to same graph will corrupt it
Search is substring only, case-insensitive — no semantic/vector search
Project detection: scans up 5 directory levels for .git, package.json, .aim
File starts with {type:_aim,source:mcp-knowledge-graph} safety marker — do not remove

name: sequential-thinking_skill
observations: Documents the sequential-thinking MCP server's 12 tools for structured analysis
Branching requires saving thought_id from add_thought return value — cannot branch without it
merge_branch target_thought can be empty string to merge into latest thought
session_type:coding triggers explore_packages automatically
Server auto-loads last session on startup — check analyze_session before start_session

name: repomix_skill
observations: Documents repomix MCP tool for codebase packing
Critical: outputId from pack_codebase is dead in subprocess mode — always read outputFilePath directly with Read tool
compress:true strips comment blocks but preserves structure and key bodies

name: detect-roles
observations: Deterministic project-type detection from file-presence signals (no LLM inference)
Checks: package.json, frontend frameworks, backend frameworks, DB signals, AI/ML packages, Cargo.toml, go.mod, Dockerfile, dbt
Writes .aim/roles.json with project_type and roles array after user confirmation
First-match classification: web-fullstack > web-frontend > web-backend > ai-ml > systems > infra > data-pipeline > general

name: init-project
observations: Generates CLAUDE.md using external AI CLI (codex→qwen→kimi→claude inline)
Skips if CLAUDE.md already exists
Falls through to claude inline if all CLIs unavailable

name: startup
observations: Session initialization: loads .aim/ context, detects roles, runs codebase-analyst if needed
Designed to be run at start of each session on a project

name: 01-small-project_api_chat
observations: src/app/api/chat/route.ts — streaming AI chat endpoint
POST handler streams Claude responses for UI component generation
Uses Anthropic API — requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in .env
Without API key, falls back to static fake code generation

name: CLAUDE.md_hierarchy
observations: Three-file hierarchy: ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md (global) > project CLAUDE.md (team) > CLAUDE.local.md (personal)
CLAUDE.md committed to source control, CLAUDE.local.md gitignored (personal overrides)
@-syntax in CLAUDE.md inlines referenced file contents into every session
# command in interactive mode merges instruction into CLAUDE.md automatically
Loading order: global → project → local — later files can override earlier ones

name: hooks_system
observations: Hook types: PreToolUse, PostToolUse, UserPromptSubmit, SessionStart, SessionEnd, Stop, SubagentStop, Notification, PreCompact
Exit codes: 0=allow, 2=block (PreToolUse only) — PostToolUse exit 2 signals Claude but does not undo the tool execution
stdin JSON schema varies by hook type AND by matched tool — stop_hook_active field only in Stop hook
settings.json hook paths: recommend absolute paths for security; $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR is the portable workaround
matcher uses | as OR operator (e.g. Edit|Write|MultiEdit), * matches all tools
PostToolUse hooks run AFTER the tool has executed — cannot block the operation, only provide feedback

name: github_actions_pattern
observations: GitHub is an async event bus for AI agents — any actor with write access can trigger any listening workflow
Multi-agent setup: separate workflow files with separate trigger phrases (@claude, @deepseek, etc.)
allowed_tools must be exhaustively listed in CI — no interactive approval mechanism
actions:read permission required to enable CI failure auto-fix tools
Agents chain: one agent's PR/comment output can trigger another workflow
1-5 minute latency per round-trip; 2000 free private GH Actions minutes/month

name: aim_directory
observations: .aim/ is the project-local persistence layer for the AI agent system
Contains: memory.jsonl (knowledge graph), roles.json (project roles), results/ (task outputs)
memory.jsonl: JSONL with safety header, entity and relation records
roles.json: {project_type, roles:[{id, preferred_tool, task_types}]}
results/{task_id}.json: {task_id, role, tool, status, output, files_changed, commit_sha}
context-loader.sh reads .aim/ on session start and injects into Claude context
Commit memory.jsonl to share team knowledge; gitignore results/ if output is transient
